h1.en: Free Batch File Converters — Process Multiple Files at Once

description.en: Convert, compress, or resize multiple images and PDFs simultaneously. Batch processing tools that work in your browser — no upload, no software.

intro.en: Stop converting files one at a time. Our batch tools let you drop multiple files, process them all simultaneously in your browser, and download results as a ZIP file. Everything runs locally — no files uploaded to any server.

question.en: How many files can I convert at once?

answer.en: Most batch tools accept up to 20 files simultaneously. All files are processed in parallel in your browser for maximum speed.
Yes. All batch conversion tools are free with no file count limits within the per-session cap.

question.en: How do I download batch results?

answer.en: After processing, all converted files are automatically packaged into a ZIP file for single-click download.

h1.en: Browser-Only File Converters — No Upload, No Server

description.en: File converters that run 100% in your browser. No files are uploaded — all processing happens on your device using JavaScript and WebAssembly.

intro.en: Traditional file conversion tools upload your files to a server, process them, and send results back. Our converters work differently — all processing happens in your browser using WebAssembly and JavaScript. Your files never leave your device.

answer.en: It means all file processing happens in your web browser using JavaScript and WebAssembly. No files are sent to any server. Your data stays on your device.

question.en: Are browser-only converters as good as desktop software?

answer.en: For most everyday tasks — converting images, compressing PDFs, formatting code — browser-based tools produce identical results to desktop software.
Modern browsers support WebAssembly, which lets us compile native code (like image processing libraries) to run directly in the browser at near-native speed.

h1.en: Privacy-First File Converters — Your Files Never Leave Your Device

description.en: Convert files privately. All processing happens in your browser — your documents, images, and code are never uploaded to any server.

intro.en: When you upload files to a cloud service, you're trusting them with your data. Our privacy-first converters eliminate that risk entirely. Every conversion runs locally in your browser. We literally cannot see your files — there's no server receiving them.

question.en: How can I verify my files aren't being uploaded?

answer.en: Open your browser's DevTools (F12), go to the Network tab, and watch for any requests when you convert a file. You'll see zero file upload requests — only static asset loads.

question.en: Is this safe for confidential documents?

answer.en: Yes. Because no data is transmitted, there is no risk of interception, data breach, or unauthorized access to your documents.

question.en: Do you store any information about the files I convert?

answer.en: No. We cannot store what we never receive. No file data, metadata, or content is ever transmitted to our servers.

h1.en: Offline File Converters — Works Without Internet After First Load

description.en: Convert files without an internet connection. Load the tool once, then disconnect — all processing continues to work offline in your browser.

intro.en: On a plane, in a remote location, or with a spotty connection? Load our tools once and they continue to work offline. All file processing happens in your browser's JavaScript engine — no internet required after the initial page load.

question.en: How do I use these tools offline?

answer.en: Simply open the tool in your browser while you have an internet connection. After the page loads, you can disconnect and all file conversion features will continue to work.
Yes. All image and PDF tools are fully offline-capable after the first page load. Developer tools are also offline-capable.

question.en: Can I save these tools to use offline permanently?

answer.en: Yes. Add the page to your browser bookmarks after it loads. As long as the browser keeps the page cached, it will work offline.

h1.en: High-Quality File Converters — Lossless & Controlled Compression

description.en: Convert images and PDFs at maximum quality. Our quality-focused converters let you control compression levels and choose lossless output where available.

intro.en: Not all converters are equal. Ours let you control output quality precisely. Use the quality slider to balance file size versus fidelity. Choose lossless formats like PNG or WebP (lossless mode) for images where quality is non-negotiable.

question.en: What quality setting should I use for the best results?

answer.en: For JPG: 85–95 gives near-lossless quality at reasonable file sizes. For WebP: 80–90 is usually optimal. For screenshots and graphics, use PNG (lossless) instead.

question.en: What is the difference between lossless and lossy compression?

answer.en: Lossless compression (PNG, WebP lossless) preserves every pixel exactly. Lossy compression (JPG, WebP lossy) reduces file size by discarding some detail — the quality slider controls how much.

h1.en: Fast File Converters — Instant In-Browser Processing

description.en: Convert files instantly. Our browser-based tools process files locally — no upload wait, no server queue, no download delay. Results in seconds.

intro.en: Cloud converters make you upload, wait for a server queue, and download results. Our tools process files instantly in your browser. A 5MB image converts in under a second. A 10-page PDF compresses in two seconds. No waiting.

answer.en: Most conversions complete in under 2 seconds for typical file sizes. Large batch operations (20 files) may take 5–10 seconds depending on your device.

question.en: Why are browser-based converters faster than upload-based tools?

answer.en: No upload or download time. The file goes directly from your storage to your browser's processing engine to the output — all on your device. No network roundtrip.
